I'm excited for everyone on the line tonight. We've got a ton of people. We had well over a thousand registered for this thing. We've got a ton of people on the line. And that was kind of the turning point in us going from spending type things to trying to build a business, trying to make money. And so this is kind of a funny picture of my wife when she had to cut up all her favorite credit cards, her favorite rule of friends. Now about this time, my dad gave me a gift, he gave me a gift and it was a book called Rich Dad Poor Dad. Hopefully you guys have read this book. If not, please go out to Barnes and Noble tonight, buy it. It's like 10 bucks. It's an amazing book. So my dad bought me this book and said on my bookshelf for a couple of weeks. One night I couldn't sleep. So my wife and I fell and sit next to me. I opened the book and I started reading and I got obsessed. I got sucked into what he was talking about. And I ended up reading the entire book in one night. And about four or five in the morning, I just sat there after I finished. I said, this, this is amazing. I just sat there. I kept thinking about everything I had learned in this thing. And about seven or eight o'clock in the morning, my wife woke up and she looked over and she saw me sitting there. I kind of started a letter and she was like, what are you doing? I said, Collette, we are buying a house. And she said, what, how are we going to buy a house? I said, I don't know yet, but we're going to buy a house. In fact, we're going to be buying a duplex. And she was like, why in the world are we going to buy a duplex? And I said, I kind of explained to her what I read about Rich Dad Poor Dad. She said, Russell, we don't have any money to buy a duplex. And I said, that's not important. I said, there's part in the book where it's kind of a funny story, but Rich Dad Poor Dad, the guy who's talking about the mentality between rich people and poor people. He says that there's, there's a mental block between rich people and poor people. So the poor people look at something they want to buy and they say, hey, I cannot afford that. He says, rich people look at something they want to buy and they say, how can I afford that? It's a very small thing. Poor people say, I cannot afford that. Rich people say, how can I afford that? And so I told my wife that story. I said, I don't know how we're going to do it, but we're going to do it. I'm going to figure out a way to afford this because I know this is what we need to be doing. And so we went out there and did a whole bunch of stuff. And eventually we were able to buy our first duplex. And this is the house we found. And we ended up living in the top part of the house and we reached out to the basement. A little while later, this became my, the basement became my office later on. And that's where the company kind of started was in the basement of this house here. So that's kind of how I got started. And it's kind of a fun process. Now, that mentality that I learned from the book Rich Steps, Poor Dad, about figuring out how I can afford things affecting me for the rest of my life. In fact, here's another picture. While I was in that house, I was trying to get into internet marketing. I was really struggling trying to figure out how to make it work. And at the time, there was a course that came out. And this is a picture of the course. It was called, it was called the Mark Joyder Farewell Package. It was a thousand dollars. For me at the time, that was just a fortune. I had no way I could afford that. And I just showed my wife thing. I said, if I get this course, it's going to change my life because I'm going to have Mark join her systems. The exact systems he used to run his business. So these systems work millions and millions of dollars. He's going to sell to me for only a thousand dollars. If I can get these systems, it'll change my business. She said, Russell, we don't have a thousand dollars. And I said, I understand that, but remember the house? How can I afford that? And I went on, I figured out a way to earn the money so I could afford the product. And this product I bought really did change my business. It was the turning point for me, trying to figure out how to make money. It's actually building a business and making money with it. Now, all of us out here that are listening, you guys are all entrepreneurs. Entrepreneurs are kind of a strange breed. And I'm sure that all your friends and family are probably thinking the same thing. Every time you're telling them how much money you're going to make, you're building this business, they think you're weird. Entrepreneurs, we have a special gift. And I don't know if any of you guys like to show heroes. This is my favorite show right now. But what it's about is all these people out there, they have some kind of special talents and abilities that one person can, excuse me, can fly. One person has ability to heal really fast. And everyone's got these different talents, these different gifts, and they can use them for bad or for good. Those entrepreneurs, we're just like that. We're just like the people in heroes. We have a gift. It's a very special gift that has a lot of power. And because of this gift, we have to learn how to use it the right way. The first part of our gift is we are responsible for creating value in the world. It's not for us. If it's not for the entrepreneurs, there's not going to be value created. Cars were not created because someone wanted to drive it, to have something to drive around. Cars were created because somebody wanted to figure out a way to make money. They built a business and an entrepreneur created this type of thing. Entrepreneurs are creating value. They're creating products. They're creating services because they want to learn how to build businesses. And the products and services they're creating are changing the world. You look at all the products and services you use every day. You think this only made the iPod, but hey, that'd be kind of fun to have a thing in the list and to use it. No, it's created by entrepreneurs who are trying to build a business to find out new, unique, innovative ways to change people's lives. And that's what they did. So us as entrepreneurs, that's part of our gift is we are responsible for creating value in the world. We're the ones that drive the change and the value for people. The next thing is that we have a responsibility to create jobs and opportunities. If it's not for entrepreneurs, there are no jobs out there. You take the entrepreneurs out of the equation and the entire country, the entire economy collapses. That's one of our jobs is to create jobs and opportunities. There's a lot of ways we can do that, not necessarily by hiring people, but we're in charge of creating jobs and opportunities. Next thing is we're in charge of stimulating the economy. Here in America, and I know you've got a lot of people from different parts around the world, but here in America, our economy is really struggling. Now it's not the government that's going to fix the economy. It's entrepreneurs are going to stimulate the economy. We're the ones that have to save this and have to protect the country, protect the economy. It's not for us to stimulate the economy, it's going to collapse. Now there are two ways we stimulate the economy. Number one is we have a role to earn as much money as possible. This is important because when we earn money, it gives us the ability to create jobs. It gives cash flow moving, it causes a whole bunch of really cool stuff. We have responsibility to make as much money as possible. The next thing is we have responsibility also to spend as much money as possible. It may seem kind of backwards from what most people are used to because most people, you've been raised your whole accent, hey, you should not spend money, you should be saving money. As entrepreneurs, it's important for us to be spending money. If we don't spend money, we're not going to, excuse me, people can't, I mean, it's us spending money that creates jobs that stimulate the economy. I remember after September 11th, and everyone remembers that whole tragedy that happened. I remember for the next couple of weeks of the government, every single day was on TV saying things like, you need to go out there and buy things, don't stay in your house and hide the money, go out there and spend money. It's going to stimulate the economy and keep things moving and growing. And people did out there, went out there and started doing that. In fact, I remember my mom, she came home at the end of one day, I got home from wrestling practice or something, and she said, hey, Russell, look, I bought a new table today. And I said, why did you buy a table? She said, because I'm out there stimulating the economy. That's what my job is to do. And I said, you know what, mom, good for you. And that's a big role. If we are not willing to spend money, it's not going to stimulate the economy and jobs are going to be lost. Think about it. Think about how many jobs are created when you purchase a new house or when you buy a new car or whatever you do. Think about how many jobs are created when you're doing that kind of stuff. So if us as entrepreneurs are making a whole bunch of money and spending a whole bunch of money, those kind of things just shrivel up and die. So kind of a fun gift we have is we have the responsibility to spend a lot of money. So for your wife or husband or spouse, whoever says, no, we need to save money, not spend it. I'm giving you guys the right ability to go out there and spend money because that's what we need to do as entrepreneurs to build the economy and help stimulate it. Okay. All right. Now I'm going to get into the actual hardcore information from this presentation. Again, like so there's a lot of you guys here that are here for different reasons. What I want to do, I want to lay out the anatomy of some successful business models. When you understand this, you'll see how your business kind of ties into this, depending on what you what you're doing. Okay. So the first thing is the anatomy of a successful information product business. Okay. There's a couple of things you need. First thing you need is some kind of product. Okay. So here's an example. One of my products is called how to make a potato gun. This is one of, you know, for those who don't know me, I'm from Idaho and one of the fun things we do during our free time is make potato guns. And I found out one day that there was a huge market for potato guns apparently. And so I created my own information product. So first thing you need is some kind of information product. Okay. Second thing you need is a sales letter to sell that product. Okay. A lot of people think that they create a product and people are going to start buying it. You have to have something there is going to sell the product. So a website that sells the product. And then the third thing you need, and by far the most important is traffic. You need to drive traffic to that website. And then people will see the, see the site that sells the product and they're going to buy the product. And that is really the anatomy of the successful information product business. There's a couple of things that gather. There's continuity and there's back end. Okay. Continuity is something monthly that you're getting, you're billing someone for back end is after they buy the initial product, we're more products and services you're going to sell them. But that is the, the anatomy of the successful info product business. You guys probably see this every single day, but it's very simple. You get a product, a website to sell the product and you need to drive traffic. And one thing you'll notice there is that I've got five different lines for representing traffic. And that's because I never want to rely on one type of traffic source. Okay. A lot of friends who put all of their, you know, all of their eggs in one basket, they drive all their traffic through paper click or through SEO or things like that. And what happens is that their business becomes very unstable because if something changes, the algorithms change, Google changes what they're doing, their entire business can be lost overnight. Okay. So it's very, very important that you are doing more than just one type of traffic. And we're going to talk about that here in a minute, but it's important to have a lot of types of traffic coming in to protect your business. Okay. Next thing I want to show you is the, in fact, before I go into that, I got kind of a funny, a funny story. You know, I, first time I showed this to Mr. Pio, that's not that, that, that seems too easy. You make it sound so simple, Russell. And it really is. You get a product, website and traffic. Those are the three pieces that's all you need to make any business successful. And anyway, it's kind of a funny story. One day, about a year and a half, maybe two years ago, I decided I wanted to see if I could get some free, some people working for me for free. So I sent an email out to my list and said, hey, I'm looking for someone who wants to do an internship. What it's going to work is you fly out to Boise, you have to pay your own way, you work for free for a month, and, and I'm not going to pay you a penny the whole time. And I've kind of laughed at it, getting what I take advantage of that. And then the next morning, I woke up to about five or six phone calls from this kid out in Florida and said, Russell, I'm the guy I want to do. I'm going to leave my wife, my kids, everything. I'm going to come out there to Boise. I want to work for you for a month for free. And I thought, wow, this guy's crazy. I'm going to let him do that. So he came out and he said, you work for a, for free for a month. And it was great. He helped, you know, he helped our company a lot. He, and we helped him a lot. He learned a lot of good stuff and he had a really good time. And the last day, I was leaving, I said, hey, I want to invite you over to dinner with, to my house. So I invited him to dinner. We're driving back to my house and the drive to my office is about 20 minutes long and we're driving it. He said something that I thought was going to be very profound. He said, you know what, Russell, I've been sitting here for the last month watching your business, watching what you do. He's like, I realized something. And I said, what was that? He says, I realized that you're really not that smart. And I sat back and I said, what? He said, yeah, you're really not that smart. All you've done, Russell, is you created a very simple system and you do it over and over and over again. He's like, I always thought there was some big, super secret or complicated thing. All you have is a very simple system you do over and over and over again. And so I kind of took it as a compliment after he told me I wasn't that smart. But the key is really that it's not that difficult. It's a simple system you do over and over and over again. Okay. So again, that is the information marketing business. Very, very simple. And Gary, you've done it a million times. I know a lot of our friends have done it. It just works very, very well consistently. Okay. Next business I want to diagram is the anatomy of a successful network marketing business. Okay. And I'm not sure I'm sure a lot of you guys out there involved network marketing. I'm involved in a couple of cuppings, make a lot of money from them. But the model for there is very similar. Okay. What you need is first off, you need a product. Second off, you need a sales letter to sell the product. And then guess what comes next? You need to drive traffic to the sales letter. Okay. And one thing you want to know about network marketing is you never, never, never want to drive traffic to the sales letter of the actual network marketing business. Their sales letters will, their websites will never sell. So what you do is you create your own information product. You set up your own website, you drive traffic. Somebody buys that information product from you. That person now becomes a lead. Now you can call them and sell them on your continuity, your back end, your continuity is the network marketing program. Okay. This is the way we're doing it. Our network marketing business is most of our students that are successful since exactly the way they do it. They create an information product and they sell that person who buys it becomes a lead and they call a person to sell them on the continuity program. It works beautifully. Okay. But again, it looks very, very simple. It's not a difficult system. It's very, very easy. Okay. Next thing anatomy of a successful physical products business. A lot of people that say, well, Russell, I'm not selling info products. I'm not selling network marketing products. I'm selling a real product. Okay. It's, it's jewelry or it's supplements, river it is. Okay. But guess what? The model is the same. You need a product, you need a website, you need to drive traffic that site. They need to have some continuity back in case one of my friend sites, he sells supplements. He has an auto ship program. That's his continuity and they sell supplement packs for his back end. Okay. Again, looks very similar. It's the exact same model. Okay. Last thing people say, well, Russell, I run an offline business. It does not work. I want to show you guys a story about one of my students, one of my close friends actually. He started a law-mowing company here in Boise. Okay. So that was his product was mowing lawns. You guys say, well, how in the world Russell, am I going to make a business online mowing lawns? Okay. And this guy built his entire business online. What he does, he, he services, he gives mowing lawns. Instead of a website giving away a free lawn mowing, all you need to do is come fill this form and he'll come mow your lawn for free and he drives traffic to that website locally. Okay. And in one summer or about a summer and a half, he was able to add almost 100 counts of just driving traffic from Craigslist and other local sites like that and built a very, very nice lawn mowing business. Now, what's interesting is that someone says, well, in the lawn mowing business, how do you have continuity? How do you have back end? When he comes to their house and mows them along for free, they sign them up for a contract. Every single month he comes back and mow their lawn. And he said, well, how do you get a back end? And the exact same thing when he's out there mowing the lawn, they walk around, they look at the, they look at the people's weeds in their yard, they look at their, you know, their trees, look at everything and after they mow the lawn, they walk up to the door, they knock on the door, they said, hey, after mowing your lawn, you know, we noticed there's things wrong with your, with your yard, with your lawn. If you like, we could do some weed killers and extra 40 bucks and we can trim your trees and extra 200 bucks so we can pick the weeds for, and they sell them all the stuff on the back end. It becomes a very, very powerful profit center. Okay. So the exact same business model works. Okay. It doesn't matter what business you're in. That's the, excuse me, that's the model. Okay. So what are the constants and all four of these business models? Okay. The constants are simple. Every single one of them has a product or service. If you don't have a product or service you're selling, you will not make money on the internet or offline. Okay. There's not to be your own product. You need a product or service you're selling. Okay. Second off, you need a website with the goal to generate leads. That's the only goal of that website is to generate leads and then you have traffic, multiple streams of traffic coming to that site and boom, if you have those three pieces, you can make money all day long in any industry, in any, doesn't matter how good the economy is, how bad it's doing, that's all you need to make money online. Okay. Very, very simple. Okay. I want to tell you guys a story about, and I'm sure a lot of you guys are at this point. When I got started with this whole business, I was learning how to do it myself. Okay. I started learning how to design websites and then how to do the, you know, set up a blog and how to write a sales letter, how to create a product. I was doing like a thousand things. I felt like I was juggling about a thousand, you know, a couple of dozen different things. And at the time it was very hard. If I felt like I ever stopped, I felt like I was going to drop something and, you know, something was going to fall apart in my business. And now this time my wife and I decided to go on a trip to Lake Powell with her family out on the house boat. So we're driving out of town and the way out of town we stopped by Barnes and Noble. And I grabbed a book and the book I grabbed is called The E-Mitt. You guys may have seen me before. It's written by Michael Gerber. And I saw that it said The E-Mitt. That must be about internet marketing. That was kind of how naive I was at the time. And I bought it. We drove down, you know, about 10 hours of Lake Powell, got into the houseboat, drove out in the middle of Lake Park, the boat. I played out, opened the book, I started reading it and I realized that about a page that had nothing to do with internet marketing. I thought, oh great. I was stuck in the middle of a lake for a week. My only book is that nothing new with internet marketing. I started reading this book and in the very beginning what he talks about is entrepreneurs. It's called The E-Mitt stands for the entrepreneur myth. And it's the myth that entrepreneurs are good at running businesses, okay? And it goes into the story about this business owner who starts this business and becomes good at everything. He starts doing everything. And at the beginning of the point of the juggling everything so hard, you know, the juggling a thousand different things, it's like they stop, the entire business is going to collapse. And I read that and I said, oh my gosh, that sounds exactly like me. I was sitting here in the houseboat freaked out because I knew the customers were sending me emails and I had no way to respond to them. I knew that I didn't know if my traffic campaigns were still going. I didn't know if anything was happening. I was scared to death. I said, that is me to a T. And it scared me. And I thought, man, let me keep reading this book to find out how they solve this problem. And he goes on to talk about the way that good businesses are built. He says, the way good businesses are built are not by having an entrepreneur running a million different things. That's not the role of the entrepreneur. He said, the role of the entrepreneur is to get the specialized knowledge and learn the stuff and then to create systems and then give employees the systems that they can go and run and manage. And he shared an example of McDonald's. He said, why do you think McDonald's is such a huge company? That it's not because their hamburgers are good. In fact, their hamburgers are pretty bad. The reason why they're good and why people pay a million dollars for a McDonald's franchise is because they have created their business. So they have all these little tiny systems that are running in the company where they can go and they can hire any kid off the street, pay them minimum wage. This person can do the functions of McDonald's and run this business. It doesn't matter if they speak English or they don't speak English. It doesn't matter. I mean, you could pretty much hire any monkey off the street and they can walk into McDonald's and run the systems perfectly. And that's why they've been able to grow out. That's why people pay so much for McDonald's franchise because they have these systems that run it. Excuse me, how do I get a drink there? And so I thought that I said, oh my gosh, that's beautiful. All I need to do is create little systems for all these things I'm juggling. If I'm juggling copywriting, I create a copywriting system and give somebody. If I'm juggling pay per click or SEO or whatever thing I'm learning about or I'm trying to do, instead of me juggling this thing and hoping that it works, I just go, I create a system and I give it to somebody else now to run advantage. And I make these systems so simple that any monkey off the street can come and run these things. And that's the power of developing systems. And I read that. I said, that's the key. That's the key from going from what I'm doing right now where I feel like I'm juggling a million things to actually building a business that's going to make me money whether I'm there or not. And so that was the one of the big ah-has for me. So the role of the entrepreneur is not to be doing everything in your business. The role of the entrepreneur is way different. It's to, number one, get specialized knowledge. Number two, to create a system. And number three, is to take action. That's our roles. As entrepreneurs, that's our roles. Not to be doing the work, but to be getting the specialized knowledge, creating systems and taking action upon it. So I want to show you how far I've taken this to an extreme. Some people think that I'm kind of crazy with this, but I took that literally and I said, okay, I'm going to go out and get as much specialized knowledge as possible. And here's a picture of my library. This is a portion of all the books and the courses and the CDs and the DVDs Eric can't buy. Okay. I buy every single product and service ever comes out because I know that I can take that content. I can learn it. I can create a system. I can give it to somebody else to go and run and manage for my business. Okay. So my job is to go and learn, and study, and have fun, and then to create systems and give no other people to run. Okay. So I buy it again, every single product and service is out there. And a lot of people say, well, Russell, yeah, sure. I'm sure you buy all the products. You're probably buddies of people native to you. And that's not true. That library right there, I personally spent over $200,000 of my own good, harder money on products, books, DVDs, courses, CDs, and that kind of stuff. Okay. And there's a reason why. Okay. Let me show you the reason. The reason is there's a guy named Mark Schultz. Some of you guys may or may not know him. Mark Schultz was an Olympic wrestling champion. So that's what we're talking about. The rest of this call is systems. I want you guys to learn that. This moment it drills your heads, you need to learn how to develop powerful little systems and then outsource these things. You're not the ones doing all the work. The cool thing is that you don't physically have to do anything to drive traffic. It's something you can outsource 100% of what other people do for you. That's very, very powerful. Creating the system, that's what we're starting about. The way I learned how to do this is I had a friend who was using a software program that I wanted to use. I called him up. I said, hey, can you teach me how to use the software? He said, wow, Russell, took us four or five months to learn how to use it. It's very complicated system, a lot of things. I don't really have time to show it to you. He said, okay, that's fine. Hold on a second. He put a couple of buttons on his keyboard, click send, and he said, check your email. Open the email, and there are about 10 of these little five-minute videos that walked me through the system. I watched them. He created these little videos for his employees. What happens is when I hire an employee, I give them these videos. Now they can use the software. I don't have to re-teach them over and over and over again. I thought, oh my gosh, that is powerful. I just need to create little videos, little system videos out of everything we're doing and then give that to people when we hire them. So what we did is there's a free software called Jing. It's at Jingproject.com. It's 100% free. I think I have a picture of it here. It's a picture of the site. You go there, download it for free, and this is the most powerful system creation software in the world. First off, it's free. Second off, it only allows you to make short five-minute videos. This is one of the keys to systems. Most people that are doing systems wrong, what they do is that they go and they try to create systems that explain to the people who are going to be watching them how they work. I go here because blah, blah, blah, and 10 minutes later, half an hour later, two hours later, they've got this video. I'm trying to hire some. They've got to watch a two-hour video. For some of your hiring from running a system, it does not matter if they understand why. That's one of the keys. They do not need to know why they are doing something. All they need to know is how. So we create a very short five-minute video showing them how to do it. I never tell them why, just tell them how. That is one of the keys here. So Jing is powerful because it makes you only have five-minute videos. It makes you condense down and shrink it down to how and not why. The next part here is do not teach again. Do not teach in your system videos. Just tell them how to do it. The system is going to be so simple that anybody could do it. So you can hire any monkey off the street and say, hey, here's a banana. Push some buttons and you're going to drive some traffic. That's how simple your systems need to be. Very, very simple so anybody in the world can do it. Now I want to show you an example how powerful this is. A couple of weeks ago, the guy who was running my SEO division of my company came in and said, hey Russell, I'm just kind of tired of working. I was basically one of my five core traffic systems. The guy who was running one of my five core traffic systems, he was gone. I thought, what in the world am I going to do? If most companies, if they lost one of their core traffic, that's enough to collapse an entire company. But what happened is I sat there, I said, wait a minute. When he was working here, he created traffic systems for every single thing he was doing, every time he learned something he created a system for, I still have these systems. So our company did not miss a beat. I was able to hire somebody else, plug them into that position, and the person now did not even have to know SEO. It was a lot cheaper to hire this person. Now step number three here is you need to understand the web 2.0 sites. Because a lot of good sites, I'm going to show you four of them. One of them is squadoo.com. One is hubpages.com. One is youtube.com. One is weekly.com. Okay. These are web 2.0 sites. Now, what you need to understand about these web 2.0 sites is that for whatever reason, Google has this relationship with them where they love them. Okay. It says squadoo. We love you guys so much. Anytime you put a page up in Google, we're going to give you tons of credit and we're basically going to put you on the first page of Google just because we like you so much. Okay. Same thing for hubpages, for YouTube, for Weebly. These are web 2.0 sites. Again, for whatever reason, Google loves them. Okay. Now, the coolest thing about all four of these sites is they allow you to put your content on their sites. So I can go to squadoo.com and say, hey, I want a page on your site. And then let me do it for free. Okay. Now, because Google already loves squadoo, that page I just put on their site is now getting listed very, very high in the search engine. Very quickly, okay. Because we're leveraging off of these sites, this site's credibility with Google. Okay. So that's the next thing. You take your article and you post on these sites. Okay. In squadoo, you post, they call it the lens, but all this is the page. You create a lens. Hubpages, you create a page. YouTube, you upload a video. Weebly, you create a page. You create pages on these sites that Google already likes, knows, and trusts. Okay. You put your article on there. So that's step number three. Okay. Step number four, there are these content sharing sites like dig.com, mix.com, shout wire, propel, or these are all content sharing sites. Okay. If you go to these sites, you can find all these different articles and stories. You can go in there and if you like an article, like for dig, for example, say you like an article, you can go and you can dig it. Say, I dig it. That's a cool article and you vote on it. Okay. And you can get a lot of traffic people digging your stuff, but that's not why we're posting the content sharing sites. Okay. These sites don't care about our articles. We're not going to get dug because articles we wrote are bad. Remember, we wrote so horrible little articles. We still want to submit them to these sites because these sites, the search engines love them and it's going to get the search engines coming back. It's going to get links from these sites first off and search engines coming back to our sites. It's going to rank our sites very fast, very, very quick. Okay. And again, you don't need to know the problem with just six behind it. Just know that it works. Okay. That's step number four and that's step number five. There's all these sites from giving RSS feed. Okay. And as you know, RSS feed is if you're on anyone's blog, you see a big orange thing like this, that's an RSS feed. Okay. So you create a page on, on Squidoo or any sites that's giving RSS feed. You take that RSS feed and you go and you submit it to a bunch of RSS directories. And this will do similar things to the content sharing sites where it gets you inbound links. It gets, it gets, how much you really get stuff like that. It gets the search engines coming back to you. Boom, it's going to list this page very, very fast. Okay. That's the open traffic method. Very, very simple. Here's the results again. Eight hours. It takes maybe, maybe 20 or 30 minutes for each QBAR to get this process done. Okay. Now the best thing about this whole thing is guess what? Your role is not to be doing any of the work. Your role is to get the specialized knowledge to understand how this thing works, create some simple systems, and take action on it. Okay. It's not to be doing keyword research. It's not to be writing articles. It's not to be posting these pages. Your goal as an entrepreneur is to be getting this knowledge, understanding this, and creating systems to get it happening. Okay. And after you have it happening, you need to step away from it. This happens every single day without you being there. Okay. You can have some goal and look on it, find the keywords, create the pages, you know, relate it back to your product, and boom, you're getting traffic without you ever doing anything. Okay. That's the key here. Okay. Traffic system number two is called the website parasite. Okay. Now for those of you who know what a parasite is, parasites will be little bugs that hook onto things and basically live off of a host. Okay. So an example, actually Gary, your seminar, Michael Pendlin shared this example. I believe it was a horse or a cow or something. So a parasite will hook onto a horse or a cow, and then the horse will go down and it'll eat, and it'll feed itself, and then it also feeds the parasite. Okay. So parasites, they're not out there going and trying to find its own food. It hooks onto a host and then it sucks the life out of the host. Okay. Now how does this relate to traffic? You know, I had a friend, actually Gary, your friend is from also, a guy named Stephen Pierce. He told me one time, he said Russell, we, everyone thinks they need to learn how to, to create traffic. So if you don't need to create traffic, traffic's already out there. What we need to do is mark and just find out where the traffic started. Yeah. Figure out how we can suck that traffic from somebody else's site back to ours. Okay. That's the concept of the website parasite. How do you suck traffic from sites that already have the traffic in coming back to your site? Okay. Now to learn the system is very similar to the last one. We invest in tons and tons of courses to understand this and really learn the system. Okay. Here's a couple of courses we bought to be able to learn this in mass driven. Okay. We went through the 92 DVDs, 11 CDs, 24 webinars, telecenter, 1600 pages of content, vested over almost 10 grand, and we're able to break down the four simple systems I'm going to share with you guys right now. Okay. So again, I'm doing you guys a lot of effort by knocking out a whole bunch of study so you can skip to the core of what actually works. Okay. So website parasite. A couple things you need to understand. First off is who is your target market. Okay. You need to understand exactly who it is you're selling to. Okay. And it's important not to say, well, I'm selling to two women or I'm selling to men. You need to understand intimately who it is you're selling to. Okay. Because when you do that, it makes this whole process a lot easier. Okay. Now one of the examples is my friends with a copyrighted Michael Morian and he told me one day he got hired to write a sales letter for a woman, for a woman's product. And he said that, you know, I don't know how to write a woman, how to write as a woman. So he went and he wanted to get inside the mind of its potential target market. So what he did is he went to a hair salon, he sat down and got out of a notepad of paper and he got there early in the morning, sat there all day long and listened to women talking. He listened to talk about their frustrations, their things they're excited about, their fears, their hopes, and everything he said. They took notes and everything they were talking about and really tried to understand the mindset of the person he was trying to target to. He went home and he wrote a sales letter which converted amazingly because he understood the mind of the person he was trying to write to. You know, you guys need to get that intimate with who it is you're selling to. It's not just, again, selling to men, selling to kids. Who is it you're selling to? What are their hopes, their fears, the things they're excited about, the things they're depressed about? What are those things you need to understand? Because after you understand who your target market is, the next step is where these people congregate. If you understand who they are, you're not going to know where they're congregating at. Okay. And so the reason I use word congregation is when you hear about congregation, usually people think of churches. And so what happens is people are congregating together based on similar beliefs and values. So all the Catholics are congregating together. All the Mormons are congregating together. The Muslims are congregating together and they're congregating in these groups. If I was trying to sell something to Mormons or Catholics or to Buddhists or whoever it is, I would go to the Baptist, I'd go to the Baptist congregation. There are already 500 people there and boom, I've got 500 of my target markets sitting there and I can go and sell stuff to them. Okay. Now before anyone turns off the webinar, I'm not trying to tell you to sell to churches, but I wanted you guys to get an understanding of congregation. Okay. Because what's happening online is exactly like this. People are congregating together based on similar beliefs and values all over the internet. And they're congregating together on message boards, forums, and websites, on email lists based on similar beliefs and values. So if you understand who your target market is, the second thing is where is that person congregating? What sites are they already on? Okay. And that's the key to understanding the website parasite. Okay. They can be on forums, on message boards, on groups. They're on eBay, they're on Craigslist, they're on blogs, they're all over the place. Okay. So here's a couple ones we use really, really, we use all the time that kind of break down this system. Okay. First one was reforms, message boards, and groups. Now there's basically three ways that we advertise on a forum. First off, we find the forum and the first way we do is signature files. Here's a picture of a post that I made in the forum. And you notice I was talking about a topic, I was talking about the new Hackersafe logo and I didn't like it very much. And I was just kind of adding to the conversation here in the very bottom, if you look at the bottom there, underneath my name, it says, receive a free copy of the underground DVD where Russell Brunson reveals exactly how he turned simple identity to a million dollar year business. Click here. Okay. Now most forms will allow you to put signature files like this. Okay. So if I go to a forum, let's say I go to a wrestling forum, like the mat.com is a wrestling forum. There's about 30,000 wrestlers on there. If I'm selling a wrestling product, I'm posting on this thing every single day or somebody that I hire is posting every day with a link coming back to my site. There are 30,000 wrestlers already sitting on that site all day long talking about wrestling. And if I've got something interesting on my signature file, I'm going to get a lot of people to click on it and come to my site and becoming a parasite. Okay. Sucking the traffic out of the site through my signature file. That's the first way. Second way is banner ads. Here's an actual official of the mat.com. If I was selling wrestling products right now, I would be buying all these banners on the right hand side because again, there's 30,000 wrestlers there and all of them are seeing these ads on the right hand side. So I'm becoming a parasite, sucking off my target market out of the form back to my site. Okay. And the last way you can form is finding joint venture partners. That's why I found Gary. The forms are a great way. Okay. Number two is eBay. Now, eBay, the cool thing about eBay is, eBay is full of just millions and millions of buyers. Nobody goes to eBay to search and research things. There's two things you need to know about SEO. There's onsite SEO and offsite. Okay. Onsite is everything that's happening on your webpage. And offsite, everything's happening on other people's websites pointing back to your website. Okay. So two things. If you can master those two things, boom, you can own SEO. It's very, very simple at that point. Okay. So here's the onsite SEO. It's everything you know for onsite SEO. I'm going to make it as simple as possible. Okay. Get a blog. Blogs will cover about 95% of what you need for onsite SEO. Okay. For example, here's a blog post I made. My key word I was focusing on was make money online. All I did was I posted this blog post and my title is three ways to make money online. And the blog did everything for onsite you need for that keyword phrase. Okay. There's other things you can do, but that's the core. If you can get that down, you can be 99% of people out there. Okay. Next thing is offsite SEO. So what you need to do is you get tons of relevant links pointing back to that page. So example, again, here's the page. My keyword is make money online. What I have to do now is I have to get a whole bunch of links coming back there. Okay. This is the wrong way to do it. Most people get a link on somebody else's website says make money online, click here now. The blue one on my link is click here now. When they click there, it takes me to this page. That's not what I want. Okay. That's the common mistake people make. What I do want, you know, all my links to look like this. Learn how to make money online with dot com secrets now. So if somebody clicks on that blue link, what happens is they come to that page. So the blue link that I'm talking is make money online and the page is taking him to is also make money online. So I use the same keyword and link that points to the same page. Hopefully that makes sense. Okay. It's very simple. That's it. If you get those two things SEO done, you own SEO. Okay.
